DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Eric Larson, Esq., of PILLSBURY & COLEMAN, LLP Tel: (415) 433-8000 for Plaintiff/cross defendant.
Linda Oliver, Esq., of SELMAN LEICHENGER EDSON HSU NEWMAN & MOORE LLP Tel: 415.979.0400 for defendant and cross-complainant Palms Insurance Company. (via Zoom Conference)
Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR# 13859, Tel: 714-471-2597 email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com

Hearing is Held. Defendant and cross-complainant contest the court's tentative ruling. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling

CROSS DEFENDANT SUGARHOUSE DIXON, LLC's, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant Sugarhouse Dixon, LLC's demurrer to the second cause of action for reformation in the cross-complaint filed by Palms Insurance Company is sustained without leave to amend. Section 17 of the insurance policy issued by Palms plainly and unambiguously precludes Palms' reformation claim. To permit Palms to pursue its reformation claim would be re-write the policy, which this court is not permitted to do. Palms' argument that its reformation claim is seeking an "endorsement" lacks merit. An endorsement is an addition and/or deletion to the policy issued by the insurer, not a ruling by a court.

Order is submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR# 13859, Tel: 714-471-2597 email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com Reported. (302/HEK)
